references.author: CB Anfinsen
references.unstructured: Anfinsen, C. B. Principles that govern the folding of protein chains. Science 181, 223–230 (1973). Demonstration that a protein’s amino-acid sequence intrinsically encodes its native three-dimensional structure, establishing the foundational principle for all modern protein folding and design research.

references.author: B Kuhlman
references.unstructured: Kuhlman, B. et al. Design of a novel globular protein fold with atomic-level accuracy. Science 302, 1364–1368 (2003). Kuhlman et al. achieved the first atomic-level de novo design of a fully novel protein fold, providing decisive proof that computational methods can accurately encode and realize new three-dimensional protein architectures.

references.author: J Jumper

Nature 596, 583–589 (2021). Jumper et al. reported AlphaFold, a deep-learning model capable of predicting protein structures at near-experimental resolution, greatly enhancing our ability to infer protein structure from protein sequence.
